title: Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold
abstract: The shape of a protein is closely linked to its function. AlphaFold is an AI system developed by DeepMind that predicts a protein's 3D structure from its amino acid sequence. It regularly achieves accuracy competitive with experiment and has been used to predict the structures of nearly all known proteins.
methodology: AlphaFold uses a transformer-based neural network architecture trained on the Protein Data Bank (PDB). It predicts the distances between pairs of amino acids and the angles of the chemical bonds that connect them using an end-to-end differentiable model.
results: In the CASP14 competition, AlphaFold achieved a median score of 92.4 GDT. This represents a significant leap forward in computational biology, enabling large-scale structural analysis that was previously impossible.
conclusion: AlphaFold has provided a solution to the 50-year-old protein folding problem. It offers a new era of structural biology where protein shapes can be predicted in seconds rather than months of laboratory work.
references: [1] Jumper, J. et al. 'Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold', Nature, 2021.
[2] Senior, A. W. et al. 'Improved protein structure prediction using potentials from deep learning', Nature, 2020.
[3] Tunyasuvunakool, K. et al. 'Highly accurate protein structure prediction for the human proteome', Nature, 2021.
[4] Berman, H. M. et al. 'The Protein Data Bank', Nucleic Acids Research, 2000.
[5] Vaswani, A. et al. 'Attention is all you need', Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2017.
